1989_07_01-India-Mumbai-Overcoming_Lust-st2-vr01

Radhanath Swami Transcriptions Template

Meta Data v9

Event Name/Title

Overcoming Lust

Event Date

01 Jul 1989

Event Location

India,Mumbai

Event Type

Other

Transcriber

Nandrani Priya Devi Dasi

Completed

16 Nov 2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-010-Overcoming%20Lust%20(NV)-FN-dt_89_07_01.mp3

Source Video

N/A

Verse

N/A

Tags – Places

Ayodhya, Ram Sethu

Tags – Pastimes

Building Ram Setu, Vishvamitra Muni and Menaka,

Tags – Persons

Ram, Krishna Buddha Avatar, Kalki avatar, Vishvamitra Muni, Menaka, Indra, Hanuman, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Arjuna, Indra

Tags – Themes

Kama, lust, surrender, holy name

Lecture Transcription

It shows the greatness of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Sri Vishnu. It describes in this verse that one who remembers the beautiful form of the lord whose eyes resembles the lotus petals, such a person becomes purified from within and without. What does it mean to be purified? The soul is essentially pure, just as the sun in the sky is pure and the rays of the sun are life-giving and beneficial for all living beings. But when the sun is obscured by a cloud it cannot be perceived. Similarly, the beauty of the soul which is part of God cannot be appreciated as long as it is covered by the cloud of ignorance.

mamaivāṁśo jīva-loke
jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ
manaḥ-ṣaṣṭhānīndriyāṇi
prakṛti-sthāni karṣati

[Bg. 15.7]

[The living entities in this conditioned world are My eternal fragmental parts. Due to conditioned life, they are struggling very hard with the six senses, which include the mind.]

The spirit soul being part of Krishna have the qualities of Krishna although infinitesimal. Krishna, Bhagavan is infinite and the soul is infinitesimal, that means the quality of beauty. All the opulence’s of God in fact, are reflected by the soul. But this cloud of ignorance keeps the pure transcendental qualities of the soul hidden and what we see are the symptoms of ignorance. Lust - kama, anger - kroda, greed - lobha, envy - matsara, illusion and pride.

Now these enemies, the six enemies of the soul, are very powerful because they are completely empowered by maya or the illusory energy. The fact is these six enemies are unconquerable. In the Bhagavad-gita, Krishna declares:

daivī hy eṣā guṇa-mayī
mama māyā duratyayā
mām eva ye prapadyante
māyām etāṁ taranti te

[Bg. 7.14]

[This divine energy of Mine, consisting of the three modes of material nature, is difficult to overcome. But those who have surrendered unto Me can easily cross beyond it.]

Lust is the greatest enemy

This material energy and all of her agents are unconquerable very difficult to overcome. Lust is the greatest enemy. In fact, Krishna tells Arjuna, in the third chapter of Gita, Arjuna asked, “My dear Krishna, why is it that even a man who is seriously on the spiritual path is impelled as if by force to engage in sinful unwanted activities.” Sometimes with our logic, with our intelligence, with our reason we know something is not right. And we know the consequences of engaging in certain activities, but yet there is a force within us that it impels us, that forces us to engage in abominable things. Arjuna said, “What is that force?” Because you know when you're at war you have to know who your enemy is before you can conquer your enemy. Just like if in a kingdom we find out that there are some spies, amongst your ministers you have to find out which one it is, otherwise you will be defeated. So similarly, there are so many agents within our heart. Of those things within my heart what is it? Who is it that is really the enemy? Who is it? What is it that is impelling me to engage in abominable activities by force? And Krishna responds:

śrī-bhagavān uvāca
kāma eṣa krodha eṣa
rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ
mahāśano mahā-pāpmā
viddhy enam iha vairiṇam

[Bg. 3.37]

[The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: It is lust only, Arjuna, which is born of contact with the material mode of passion and later transformed into wrath, and which is the all-devouring sinful enemy of this world.]

It is kama, it is lust only Arjuna, which is born of the contact of the material mode of passion, which is later transformed into wrath, which is the all-devouring sinful enemy of this world. Now what does lust mean? Kama. some people have a very crude harsh conception that lust means some erotic relationship with a woman illicitly. That is a very, very horribly gross idea of kama. But actually, kama means any desire we have separate from God's. Ahh? Even the most pious people are victim of kama in the real sense. Even some of the most refined sophisticated and religious people are victims of kama in the true sense. Krishna says in Gita:

bhoktāraṁ yajṣa-tapasāṁ
sarva-loka-maheśvaram
suhṛdaṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ
jṣātvā māṁ śāntim ṛcchati

[Bg. 5.29]

[A person in full consciousness of Me, knowing Me to be the ultimate beneficiary of all sacrifices and austerities, the Supreme Lord of all planets and demigods, and the benefactor and well-wisher of all living entities, attains peace from the pangs of material miseries.]

That everything belongs to Me all the planets and everything within all planets is My property and you are My property and every living thing is My property and everything is meant for My enjoyment. The misconception that anything is meant for my enjoyment, to see or use anything for any other purpose than the enjoyment of God is considered by Bhagavad-gita to be kama or lust the all-devouring sinful enemy of the world. Now how to conquer this all-devouring sinful enemy? There is a method and it is a sure hundred percent method. Ahh and the Supreme Lord showed us the method in a wonderful way. What He did first He sent to this world lust personified. Who knows who that personality was? Ravana? Ravana was the most striking emblem of lust in the history of all creation. He was so blatant and so straightforward in his position.

Lakshmi Devi cannot be separated from Vishnu

Sita is the property of Rama. Sita is none other than Lakshmi Devi, the Goddess of Fortune. All the fortune of the world is is the energy of Sita, the daughter of King Janaka. What do we mean when we say Goddess of Fortune? What is fortune? Fortune does not simply mean money. Fortune means anything good. When you wish someone good fortune it doesn't mean simply you wish him a lot of money. Fortune means good health, fortune means popularity, fortune means comforts in life, fortune is good friendship, good family. It also means nice income, fortune means anything good within this world, that is good fortune. And to lose anything good is misfortune.

So, Lakshmi is the Goddess of Fortune. Anything that is good, anything that we like, is the energy of Lakshmi Devi and in one sense it is non-different than a Lakshmi Devi. Now as we know Lakshmi Devi never ever leaves the feet of Narayan. Her eternal position is at His feet, massaging Him. As a matter of fact, one name of Lakshmi is Sri, Shriji and one name of Vishnu is Srivatsa. Why is He named Srivatsa? Because Lakshmi in order to never ever be separated from Vishnu, she has appeared as a golden hair on His chest. in this form she never leaves His heart. She is closest to His heart at all times. This is also the meaning of the name Sreenivas, that Vishnu always has Sri upon his heart. Srinathji, Vishnu, the lord and the master of Lakshmi Devi. Lakshmi never leaves Vishnu.

So, what happens when we take the fortune of this world and we try to separate it from Vishnu by using it for our own satisfaction? Lakshmi expands Herself same energy, same person but she appears in her form of Durga. Durga in Sanskrit literally means prison. When you see Lakshmi, she has a lotus flower in her hand, which is the sign of auspiciousness, victory, success, beauty and opulence. In another hand she's offering her blessings. Those who approach her, by using her energy in the service of Vishnu she appears before that person in this beautiful form. But those who take the property of Vishnu, those who take her and try to use her to exploit her for their sense gratification, understand all fortune in this world is Lakshmi. When we tried to enjoy Lakshmi instead of offering her to Vishnu where she belongs and she appears Durga. Durga does not carry lotus flowers. She carries swords and trident, clubs. This trident is her most formidable weapon. It has three prongs which signified the threefold miseries of material existence, adhyātmika, adhidaivika and adhibautika. Miseries caused by one's own body and mind, such as mental distress, worrying, physical disease, old age. Miseries caused by other living beings, sometimes people insult you, sometimes people cheat you, sometimes people exploit you. Sometimes even your loved ones just completely disappoint you and adhidaivika which is miseries caused by natural disturbances, such as too much heat, too much cold, too much rain, too much wind, volcanoes hurricanes, cyclones, tornadoes. Durga Devi is constantly keeping those persons who are under her control but she keeps them in line with this trident stabbing their hearts with the threefold miseries of material existence.

So now that all fortunate things in this world’s are Laxmi Devi, now Laxmi Devi appeared as goddess Sita and everyone knows that Sita is exclusively meant for Rama. What is the definition of love? Love means to assist Sita in her affair with Rama. Is that not what Ravan, is that not what Hanuman and Lakshmana and Sugriva and all the residents of Ayodhya did. The residents of Ayodhya, their only desire was to see Sita and Rama happy, that was their only motivating force in life. They would die at any moment if it helped Sita and Rama enjoy together. They had no false eg. Their only desire was to simply see Sita and Rama together happy and therefore when they saw Sita and Rama together happy that gave them the greatest most ultimate bliss, ecstasy and satisfaction in life. The residents of Ayodhya they're blessed their happiness was incomprehensible by the material mind. But Ravana, he wanted to take Sita for himself. That is called kama or lust.

In the Caitanya-caritamrta, Krishnadas Kaviraj Goswami describes that love is the natural inclination of the soul to want to give pleasure to God. But when that natural affection for God is misplaced in trying to enjoy the property of God for our own selfish interest then that love is transformed into lust or kama. Prema and kama is the same energy. Love and lust is the same energy. It is the same inclination within us. Prema is when that energy within us of affection is placed toward God and when it is placed anywhere else is called kama or lust.

So Ravana was very tricky. How he stole Sit. He disguised himself as a Sadhu. He was a very great mystic yogi. Ravana had ten heads and he was given benediction by Lord Shankar that if anyone chopped off his heads they would grow right back. He was a terrible monstrous looking creature, of course he thought himself very handsome. But for any distinguished person he was horrible. How is it that he completely changed his form in every respects? He only had one head and he looked like a very, very gentle peaceful saint. Ahh? It is because he had mystic power. He had tremendous mystic power. He could perform incredible miracles. He was a great yogi. Of course, in today's society if someone could perform some little miracle, they are considered God. Ahh? Sometimes somebody tells me that, “My guru is God.” But in the scriptures, it explains who God is and when He appears in this world.

Who is God?

One time I was riding in a bus from Kurukshetra to Rishikesh and I was sitting next to this man and when you dressed like this naturally anyone who has any philosophical inclinations wants to tell you what they know. Ahh? You become a very, very good target for anybody who has any religious information they want to tell someone. So, this person said to me that “Ahh, My guru is God” and I just looked at him and said, “Hare Krishna.” So, then my friend, who was with me he started arguing with this person like anything, “Your guru's not God. How could he be God?” and they started getting in a very fierce argument. By this time this person hated us, because we were offending his guru. I was just looking out the window trying to see this nice tree and the Himalayas and everything else. Then my friend said, “I'm finished talking to this person, he thinks his guru is God now you tell my friend here and he'll tell you who your guru really is thank you very much.” [devotees laugh] He said, “My guru is God.” I said, “Ahh Your guru is God umm I'm not saying he isn’t God but you have to prove to me that he's God. You can’t expect me to just believe anybody's God just because you say. What is the evidence?” The first thing he did is, he said, “My guru can perform miracles.” I said, “Oh is that the sign of God? What miracles can he perform? Tells me, ‘How he could create things. He makes watches and make all different varieties of things and how he could change his form.’ I said, “Oh sounds like Ravana.” [devotees laugh] He could do all these things too. Is Ravana God? Ravana made his whole city into gold. That was a mystic power. You think anyone could just make their whole city into gold? Ravana was a ten-headed monster he could look like a very nice saintly sadhu and marichi one of his other demons he was also a rakshasa monster. He could change his form into a beautiful playful deer and we read in the story of Hiranyaksha, fighting with Lord Varaha.

Not only did he during that battle not only did he create a watch or another little gift but he created my God all of a sudden, he made mountains fall from the sky and hundreds and thousands of millions of lions and tigers and snakes just showering from the clouds and by the way tidal waves started coming at Lord Varaha. He created all this. Is Hiranaksha God? Is Ravana God? They are demons. Even a demon an asura an enemy of God through the mystic yoga process could perform miracles. Is that the sign of God? Then he began to explain but everyone accepts him as God. What do you mean everyone accepted him? I haven't accepted, I'm not everyone. I'm not saying he's not, but you just proved to me. If you can prove to me, I will worship him and surrender, abandon all varieties of religion and surrender [devotees laugh]. You have to convince me. But you know in the west we don't accept things so easy. Then he said, “Well how do you know who is God?” I said, “Oh you're asking me?” I said, “The criterion is what the scriptures say.” The scriptures tell us, when God appears, how He appears, and why He appears ahh? and without the criterion of the scripture anyone could claim they are God and how do we know. Therefore, God tells us when and how He appears. It's explained that in Kali Yuga God appears three times. Only three times!!

There may be many great gurus and make profits are representatives of God but God Himself Bhagavan only appears in Kali Yuga three times. When Krishna left this world Kali Yuga began. He appeared in Dwapara Yuga. his next incarnation is Lord Buddha, Keshava drita Buddha sarira jaya jagadisha hare” Keshava drita Buddha sarira jaya jagadisha hare jaya jagadisha hare [Sri Dahsavatara Strotra 9]. Then it describes the Lord Buddha appears in the province of Madhya Pradesh. It is describing his father's name. His father’s name is Vishnu Jyoti and it describes what His mission is. To stop unnecessary animal slaughter and to establish the principle of ahimsa. Is your Guru Lord Buddha? No, no not Buddha obviously but Buddha appeared 2500 years ago. Alright he said, “My guru must be the next one.”

kṛṣṇa-varṇaṁ tviṣākṛṣṇaṁ
sāṅgopāṅgāstra-pārṣadam
yajṣaiḥ saṅkīrtana-prāyair
yajanti hi su-medhasaḥ

[Sb. 11.5.32]

[In the Age of Kali, intelligent persons perform congregational chanting to worship the incarnation of Godhead who constantly sings the names of Kṛṣṇa. Although His complexion is not blackish, He is Kṛṣṇa Himself. He is accompanied by His associates, servants, weapons and confidential companions.]

It describes the next form of God, He appears in a beautiful golden complexion, and He is constantly with his arms raised chanting the holy names and dancing in Kirtan. And that was Lord Chaitanya, he appeared 500 years ago. He said, “Well that's not the one either, my guru doesn't look like that either” he said, “He must be the next one.” I said, “The next one is Kalki avatar.” He said, “That’s it. My guru is Kalki avatar. Yes.” Kalki avatar comes on a white horse and with a sword he cuts off the heads of the demons and he appears at the end of Kali Yuga, which happens to be four hundred and twenty-seven thousand years from today. He said, “Well, that's not my guru.” So, then he said to me, “You know, maybe my guru isn’t God” and then he asked me, “Who is God? What is God?” The answer is explained in the real qualities of God. Parasara muni, the great father of Vyasa muni describes in the shastra that Bhagwan the Supreme Lord is He who possesses all opulence’s in full

aiśvaryasya samagrasya

vīryasya yaśasaḥ śriyaḥ

jṣāna-vairāgyayoś caiva

ṣaṇṇāṁ bhagam itīṅganā

[Viṣṇu Purāṇa 6.5.47]

That He is the possessor of all wealth, all knowledge, all beauty, all fame, all strength and all renunciation. That is Bhagavan. And a real guru, is not one who claims to be God, but claims to be the humble servant, the instrument in the hands of God, to deliver the mercy of God.

Ravana: the symbol of Lust within us

So Ravana, he was worshipped in Lanka as God and he had many, many mystic powers beyond anybody today. He didn't claim only to be God. He claimed to be better than God. When Shurpanaka his sister told him about the beauty of Sita, he decided I'm going to get her for myself. I am the supreme enjoyer. No one has a right to enjoy a beautiful woman like that except me and Vibhishana and other advisers said no, no, no please Ravana, she's Rams wife, Rama is very powerful. He's God, He's Bhagavan. Ravana said I am Bhagvan. Rama is nothing I will destroy him. Everything is meant for my enjoyment ahh and therefore, he disguised himself and he stole away Sita and he brought her to Sri Lanka. We read in the Ramayan by Valmiki muni of how Lord Rama and his brother Lakshman looking for Goddess Janaki, he made alliances with Sugriva and Hanumanji and together with the great army of monkeys they attacked the island of Sri Lanka and there was a great war and ravana was so powerful that no one could defeat him. His son Indrajit was formidable in battle. He even conquered Indra himself. Between Ravana, Indrajit, Kumbhakarna and the other Rakshasas in his army even the demigods in the heavens were absolutely stripped of their pride and were living in constant fear. They were utterly unconquerable by any human, by any jivatma. Ravana was the symbol of kama and know whatever desire you have to enjoy separate from God in your heart is due to the presence of Ravana within there ahh?

When Indra understood his helpless position before the power of Ravana, Indra with all the other devatas, all the other sadhus, they approached the Supreme Personality of Godhead Rama and said, “Please only you can save us. Only you can help us. You are our only shelter.” Rama descended on this world and how this great battle with Ravana took place. Lord Shri Ramchandra with his mighty bows, he wanted to show the world how powerful Ravana really was, how powerful lust really is. Every time He cut off the heads of Ravana, another one grew. Doesn't that sound like our own material desires? [maharaja laughs] Every time you try to give up something, every time you do some yajna or some sacrifice, some puja and you give up one material sinful desire and immediately another head grows. And you think, “Oh my God” then you perform so much penance in atonement and practice yoga and you take vows to give up this, this lusty propensity in your heart inside. Finally, you conquer it you give it up and then [maharaja makes sound of a head growing] another head grows. For those on the spiritual path who are trying to give up material attachments they know that as soon as you cut down one head ten more grow ahh? It's an endless war, it’s an endless battle.

Rama wanted to show us all the nature of lust that's how he was playing with Ravana in this way by cutting off his head [maharaja making sound of heading cut and appearing]. First, they'd be cut then, and they grow. Finally, Rama he understood that I must shoot my specialist most arrow in his heart and when he cracked his heart, pierced his heart, Ravana fell to the ground. Now Rama is the only one that has that arrow. That arrow cannot be obtained by any mortal, by any jiva, by any living entity. It is the exclusive power of Rama that is why Lord Sri Krishna says in Gita:

daivī hy eṣā guṇa-mayī
mama māyā duratyayā
mām eva ye prapadyante
māyām etāṁ taranti te

[Bg. 7.14]

[This divine energy of Mine, consisting of the three modes of material nature, is difficult to overcome. But those who have surrendered unto Me can easily cross beyond it.]

That this material energy this maya, this kama is very, very difficult to overcome but one who surrenders to me, I take the war in my own hand. I will conquer that lust, I will conquer Ravana within your heart. For Me it is not difficulty and that is why Krishna says

bahūnāṁ janmanām ante
jṣānavān māṁ prapadyate
vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti
sa mahātmā su-durlabhaḥ

[Bg. 7.19]

[After many births and deaths, he who is actually in knowledge surrenders unto Me, knowing Me to be the cause of all causes and all that is. Such a great soul is very rare.]

After many births and deaths of practicing the various religious systems of the world in the different processes of yoga when one finally comes to the point of knowledge, he surrenders to Vasudev, surrenders to Krishna surrenders to Rama knowing him to be the cause of all causes and all that is. When you surrender to the lord you put your life in His hands, under His protection and He is almighty. That is the difference between bhakti and the other yoga systems. The other yoga systems by meditation, by pranayam, by yoga asanas, by the discriminatory power of jṣâna or knowledge we are trying to conquer, Ravana within our own heart.

Even Vishwamitra muni ahh? He was the greatest of all Yogi's. How great he was ahh? He lived in Gomukh. The source of the Ganges and in the winter when it's so covered with snow. We went there in the summer to bathe. Hmm, do you remember? How many have been to Gomukh, the source of Ganga? Last year a group of us went in the month of July, hottest month of the year ahh? At that place it is the hottest month, in May it's still snow. And we went to take bath and my God it's so cold ahh? Most of my friends they went in and and it’s not that they came out the coldness pushed them out ahh? Literally like launching pad, they went in and immediately after going under water they just [maharaja makes sound of being pushed out]. They just flew out back onto the rocks [maharaja makes sounds of shivering]. So, I was thinking I'm going to chant my Gayatri mantra in the Ganga. Vishwamitra Muni would stay in the Ganga for six months without being disturbed. So, I’m not Vishwamitra, but at least I can chant Gayatri mantra. So, I went in and I will tell you, it was the absolutely the fastest Gayatri mantra I've ever chanted in my life [devotees laugh]. I never knew I could utter syllables, sanskrit syllables so fast [devotees laugh]. The cold empowered me to just grrr [maharaja imitating cold weather, devotees laugh] and as soon as the Gayatri mantra was done [maharaja making sounds of coming out, devotee laugh] I flew out. It’s very cold.

Vishwamitra muni was a very great yogi for six months a year he'd be up to his neck in the Ganges, in the peaks of the Himalayas and through this process he was trying to conquer kama. Ahh? Everyone was thinking, “My God, of all Yogi's he has actually successfully conquered kama altogether.” His senses were like stone they were unphased by anything. So much so that Indra was feeling that, “My God! This man is so powerful, he may conquer my heavenly kingdom.” So, he sent down the beautiful apsara Menaka and while Vishwamitra was sitting in his samadhi, she came and she tinkled her bangles like this [maharaja making sounds of bangles tinckling] and then she tinkled her ankle bells [maharaja makes sounds of ankle bells]. When he heard this, he was thinking, “Ahh, so pleasing this sound, what is it.” And he opened his eyes, he saw this beautiful woman and when he saw her beauty what happened? From within his head one of those ten heads [maharaja immitating] came. Again, the head of lust appeared Kama, and he fell from his yogic position. You see by any yogic process by our own endeavor it is impossible for any length of time to conquer Ravana within our heart. But Krishna says,

bhaktyā tv ananyayā śakya
aham evaṁ-vidho ’rjuna
jṣātuṁ draṣṭuṁ ca tattvena
praveṣṭuṁ ca paran-tapa

[Bg. 11.54]

[My dear Arjuna, only by undivided devotional service can I be understood as I am, standing before you, and can thus be seen directly. Only in this way can you enter into the mysteries of My understanding.]

Through the process of bhakti one can understand me as I am.

sarva-dharmān parityajya
mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja
ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo
mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ

[Bg. 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.]

This is what it means. Abandon all other yoga processes, abandon in all varieties of religion and just surrender to Me. I will protect you. I will take care of you I will destroy the demons in your heart with my arrows. When we surrender to the Lord, Rama appears within our heart. I began this lecture, this evening by chanting this beautiful prayer from the Garuda Purana

Om Apavitrah Pavitro Vaa Sarva-Avasthaam Gato-[A]pi Vaa
Yah Smaret-Punnddariikaakssam Sa Baahya-Abhyantarah Shucih

[Garuda Purana]

This is what it means. That when we remember Krishna, when we remember Lord Shri Ramchandraji, he is non-different than that remembrance. The process of bhakti is man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru [Bg. 9.34].

Always Remember Krishna, never forget Krishna

To always remember the lord, to become His devotee, to worship Him and to offer your homage unto Him. Through this process, Ram appears within our heart and with His mighty arrows He shoots right into the heart of Ravana and all his heads and everything else falls and disappears from our life eternally.

So, on the tenth of this month, on the eighth of this month I think [devotee: tenth]. Ahh, tenth, we are celebrating Dussera. Sometimes called Ram Vijayotsava. It is the day when we celebrate the grand victory of Lord Ramchandra over the mighty demon asura, Ravana and of course this is a factual historical event that took place in Sri Lanka during the Treta yuga. But know that it is also a lila that can take place within your own heart when you take shelter of Lord Sri Ramachandra. Therefore, on this day and in preparation for this day we should truly meditate on what this lila means to us. What it means to our life, the symbol of kama, Ravana who was dwelling in the heart of all conditioned souls. The sinful deadly enemy of every living being can only be defeated as exhibited on this most holy day of Dussehra by the mighty glory of Rama. By remembering Rama, He appears in our heart. By remembering Him, He kills all the demons within our heart.

Therefore, Lord Kapila Deva explained that of all the scriptures throughout the world there are so many instructions, so many vows, so many rituals, so many poojas and so much philosophy but it's all meant to bring us, to simply one conclusion: to always remember Krishna and never forget Krishna. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has said,

kṛṣṇa—sūrya-sama; māyā haya andhakāra

yāhāṅ kṛṣṇa, tāhāṅ nāhi māyāra adhikāra

[CC Madhya 22.31]

[Kṛṣṇa is compared to sunshine, and māyā is compared to darkness. Wherever there is sunshine, there cannot be darkness. As soon as one takes to Kṛṣṇa consciousness, the darkness of illusion (the influence of the external energy) will immediately vanish.]

Krishna is like the Sun and Maya ignorance with all of her consorts. So, which are these enemies and these demons they are like darkness. In the presence of the sun darkness cannot exist. When we remember God with devotion in our hearts, all the bad qualities, all illusions, all ignorance is dispelled.

The names of the Lord are most powerful

And that is why it is said in Ramayana, it is said in Bhagavatam, it is said in all the great scriptures which teach the highest platform of love, prema that in this age of Kali Yuga there is no more glorious process, there is no greater benediction than the chanting of the names of God. ceto-darpaṇa-mārjanaṁ bhava-mahā-dāvāgni-nirvāpaṇaṁ [CC Antya 20.12, Śrī Śrī Śikṣāṣṭaka 1]. That the prime benediction, the ultimate mercy of the lord is his holy name kali-kāle nāma-rūpe kṛṣṇa-avatāra [CC Ādi 17.22]. The fact is that Krishna has incarnated in this age of Kali in the name. In His holy name and the name of God is so powerful, it has all the potencies of God Himself. nāmnām akāri bahudhā nija-sarva-śaktiḥ [CC Antya 20.16, Śrī Śrī Śikṣāṣṭaka 2]. All the Strength, all the beauty, all the glory, all the opulence of the lord is fully manifested within His name.

And Rama is like. Oh my god! Of course, He is God. So, he looked at Hanuman and Hanuman said, “Rama My lord, my master this is not your business,” he said, “This ocean, this material existence is like an ocean and all the fools and rascals in this material existence, they are all like rocks just falling down to the bottom of this ocean and drowning in ocean of birth, old age disease and death. Rama, you’re supposed to be in Vaikunta enjoying with Sita. Every now and then you come down and deliver the miscreants I mean you deliver the pious and annihilate the miscreants and re-establish the religious principles. But Rama you should really go to play with Sita and having a beautiful time in the spiritual world. He said that your holy name you have invested all of Your powers and all your glories in your name to constantly be within this material world to do this work. Uploading the rocks in the ocean of up lifting the fallen souls and taking them out of this material existence. Therefore, my lord your holy name is the greatest of all deliverers.

In fact, when Rama appeared how many people did he deliver? How many demons did he liberate? How many people did he send back to Vaikunta? When Krishna appeared how many, some… But in the forms of the name of Krishna and Rama throughout all the ages hundreds and millions and billions of people have gone back to the spiritual world. So therefore, your name is especially incarnated in this world to deliver the most fallen from the ocean of birth and death. And Hanuman said, “us, we are your devotees. It is our duty to just assist you in your enjoyment with Sita and let us spread the glories of the Holy name and float the rocks. This is all your pastime, of course Rama you can float rocks, you can do anything. In the form of… In the form of Sankarshan, in the form of Mahavishnu, you are creating millions and billions of universes and having each planet, each universe floating in its orbit to float a rock? You want to show us that you are empowering more of your glory for the delivering of the fallen souls in your name and in your own personal form.” This is the power of glory of the name of God. It is the supreme purifier.

So, on the day of Dussehra what we should understand is the utter importance and significance within our life to take shelter of the holy name of Sri Rama and have him once for all kill the Ravana of our heart and allow the pure love of the soul to shine for our own eternal enjoyment and for the benefit of all living beings chant Hare Krishna and be happy. This is really the conclusion of all knowledge.

Thank you very much

Any questions?

Q&A Transcription

[Maharaja:] Yes.

[Ananda Vrindavan Prabhu:] Maharaj you are saying that chanting the holy names and surrendering to the mighty lord. It so happens that the mind is so fluctuating. The level of surrender is not possible for the same time. Today I am going to fully surrender to you the mighty lord, the same thing may not happen tomorrow. My senses are so strong. How to see to it that the surrender is at the same level, it is constant, and it is forever so that my material desire does not come out at all. When the surrender comes, again, the material desire comes out. How do I know I have to surrender so that material desire doesn’t come? But since the surrender is not at the same level and is not constant, the material desire again comes out and I know it I must surrender but I can’t do it. There is some pressure. How to see the surrender is at the same level and is constant.

[Maharaja:] I’m sure everyone in the room can relate to your question. Therefore, it is

munayaḥ sādhu pṛṣṭo ’haṁ
bhavadbhir loka-maṅgalam
yat kṛtaḥ kṛṣṇa-sampraśno
yenātmā suprasīdati

[Sb. 1.2.5]

[O sages, I have been justly questioned by you. Your questions are worthy because they relate to Lord Kṛṣṇa and so are of relevance to the world’s welfare. Only questions of this sort are capable of completely satisfying the self.]

Your question is all auspicious because the answer to this question is auspicious for the development of every living being who is on the spiritual path. Ths is the condition of a neophyte devotee, a sadhana bhakta. He is constantly struggling to control the mind and the senses. Sometimes the mind is called chanchalam. Ahh? Krishna says in Gita. yato yato niścalati manaś caṣcalam asthiram [Bg. 6.26]. He says the mind is constantly wandering here and there. Whenever and wherever the mind wanders due to its flickering and unsteady nature we must drag it back to the self. This is the process of sadhana bhakti. We should not be surprised when the mind goes running here and there. It’s the nature of the mind. The difference between a Godas and a Goswami is not that the mind doesn't wander. It's the Goswami says, “No!” to the mind and the Godas says, “Very nice” to the mind. Ahh? Just like your mind may see an object to enjoy that you know it's very sinful. A Godas a servant of the senses is thinking, “Ahh! How very sweet, how very nice. It's Krishna's arrangement otherwise you wouldn't have put this object before me so let me enjoy.” Our mind justifies. When the mind is attached to the senses the mind is our worst enemy bandhur ātmātmanas tasya
yenātmaivātmanā jitaḥ [Bg. 6.6]

See how the mind works. First the senses give the indication, look at beautiful thing enjoy it, enjoy it, it's so wonderful. Your mind is thinking, “Ahh, I know I'm not supposed to do this but somehow or other I really want to do it. Therefore, I got it I know now I have it all figured out not a blade of grass moves without the sanction of Krishna and everything is his mercy, a devotee is supposed to see everything at the mercy of God. So therefore, this beautiful sense object is been given to me by Krishna and it's his mercy and it's an offence to not accept his mercy [devotees laugh]. So, therefore, let me enjoy, it's God's wish after all, God put us on this earth to enjoy and we're not perfect.” So, you followed… That is called Godas. A Goswami the same inclination is there; the same temptation is there but he's willing to take shelter of the verse of Gita. Krishna says the senses are so strong and impetuous Arjuna, that they forcibly conquer the intelligence even if a man of discrimination trying to control them but then

tāni sarvāṇi saṁyamya
yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ
vaśe hi yasyendriyāṇi
tasya prajṣā pratiṣṭhitā

[Bg. 2.61]

[One who restrains his senses, keeping them under full control, and fixes his consciousness upon Me, is known as a man of steady intelligence.]

A Goswami is one who restrains his senses in times of temptation and trial. He restrains his senses by force of his intelligence and he fixes his consciousness on Krishna. So, the question is will you be a Goswami or a Godas? That is your choice. The temptation will be there due to the natural conditioning of your mind. But Krishna says by constant practice and detachment the mind can be controlled. That is whenever the test comes before us, we must fix our mind on God and withdraw like a tortoise, withdraws his limbs within the shell, Krishna says we must withdraw by our… with our intelligence the senses from their objects. This is how you take shelter of Krishna. You have to practice and by strong sadhana under the supervision of spiritual master or the self-realized souls we can learn gradually how to control our mind and senses through this process. It is not an immediate thing. It is a gradual process of purification. The more we taste Krishna, the more we become detached from the objects of the senses. But when the tests come like you're explaining we have to constantly practice and if you have strong sadhana and if you have association with saintly persons when the test comes you will have the strength, you will have the proper intelligence, to restrain the senses and fix the consciousness on Krishna and that time chant the holy names. When you see the heads of maya coming up in your heart and you see there's a lack of surrender in your heart, you should just immediately cry out for the holy names of Krishna. Krishna you're the only one who can protect me, you're the only one who can save me.

So sadhana bhakti means steady, regulated spiritual discipline in our life and regular association with saintly persons. If we have this and we will gradually have the strength and the spiritual integrity to overcome the obstacles of this material energy and gradually become purified or surrender becomes our very nature at all times.

Is there any other questions? Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
